opus-4-6: Opus brought architectural discipline to match-3, then decorated it like a thesis defense with gems.
The state machine is impressive, but the board occasionally feels like it needs a project manager to swap two tiles.
Those section banners have more ceremony than some level-up screens.
The neon is rich, but the title still says Tile Match like the naming budget evaporated first.
Opus made every event correct, polished, and slightly afraid of spontaneity.
The special tiles work hard, even while the UI introduces them like meeting agenda items.
A hint delay that polite makes the board feel like it is waiting for written consent.
The sound design has range, though some shuffle tones arrive like a synth fell down stairs.

It is polished enough to respect and formal enough to make candy feel under review.
sonnet-4-6: Sonnet named it GEM BLITZ, which sounds fast until the single-mode structure asks everyone to queue politely.

The Courier styling gives neon arcade energy filtered through a printer manual.
The particles sparkle nicely, then the game reminds you it has exactly one main pace.
Sonnet made a smooth match-3 with the strategic range of a very shiny hallway.
The specials are fun, but the onboarding explains them like it is worried you skipped the brochure.
Mobile works, though the gems sometimes feel like luxury buttons on a tiny calculator.
The game has taste and speed, just not quite enough chaos to scare the leaderboard.

gpt-5-4: GPT 5.4 built Prism Pulse like a match-3 game escaped from a product launch.

The cascades are gorgeous, but they occasionally fight the UI for top billing.

The specials are genuinely fun once you finish walking past the lobby signage.
GPT 5.4 has excellent juice, then pours extra frosting on the frosting.

This version is powerful, polished, and allergic to leaving empty space alone.

sonnet-4-6: Sonnet's Deluxe Edition has the confidence of an arcade cabinet that hired a stylist.

gpt-5-4: GPT 5.4 built Space Invaders and then surrounded it with mission control for one spaceship.
